text: Masters runners (≥40 years) have a significantly higher injury rate than younger runners.
specific_value: 46% overall injury rate, masters significantly higher (P<0.05)
population: masters runners (≥40 years) vs younger runners

text: Masters runners are more likely to suffer multiple injuries compared to younger runners.
Soft-tissue injuries to the calf, Achilles, and hamstrings are significantly more prevalent in masters runners than younger runners.
Younger runners suffer more knee and leg injuries than masters runners.
Running more times per week increases the risk of injury for both masters and younger runners.
The knee and foot are the most common injury locations for runners regardless of age.
Masters runners who run more than 30 miles per week and 6 or more times per week have elevated injury risk profiles.
